strengths: Successfully completed all required phases including loop introduction, positioning, pusher advancement, and tail cutting
Maintained good instrument control throughout the procedure
Achieved a solid completion time of 60.25 seconds with no penalty deductions
weaknesses: Loop positioning at the marked site could be more precise based on disagreement between evaluators
Some hesitation visible during pusher advancement phase
Could improve efficiency in the tail cutting sequence

drills.name: Mark-centering drill
drills.description: Place the loop at the target mark, pause, and verify alignment before cinching. Practice 10 repetitions focusing on exact mark placement.
drills.targets_weakness: Loop positioning at the marked site could be more precise.

drills.name: Loop opening and parking
drills.description: 10 repetitions of opening the loop and parking it around the target appendage before tightening, emphasizing smooth pusher control.
drills.targets_weakness: Some hesitation visible during pusher advancement phase.

drills.name: Tail-cut finish drill
drills.description: 10 secure placements followed by controlled tail cuts without disturbing the loop, focusing on economy of motion.
drills.targets_weakness: Could improve efficiency in the tail cutting sequence.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent technique into more precise positioning and faster clean execution. Your completion time is good, but refining mark accuracy will help you approach advanced-level performance.
